feed.title: 8holic
feed.subtitle: The main hub by 8holic (Real name Bryan) to create a hub to link all existing projects and files.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 5
entry.content:
At 12am I went to hike the Fushimi Inari Shrine, I heard that this location is very busy in the day. So I decided to visit it at night. It has a completely different atmosphere, a little overworldly.

In kyoto I went to the train museum, at one segment I can pilot a toy train with a camera front looking. That was really cool

At the end I went and oversee the city, it is very urban. TBH I didn’t really feel much, the Hibike collab was nice through. i liked that show. I got a coaster.

However, I felt that I spend way too little over in Kyoto, the next time I need to plan differently.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 4
entry.content:
Kyoto 5 Day trip

I had something intresting from the supermarket, fried noodles on bread. Like hot dog. Looks weird but it taste ok, probabl wont eat again in the future

This time I am visiting Ikoma Sanjo Amusement Park, before going to Nara. I saw this location and was interested in visiting it.

Ikoma Sanjo Amusement Park is free to enter, where I pay for the rides I took. Being located at the top of the mountain also means there is some nice sights.

One cuorsity is the “White house”. When I went initally there wasn’t anyone, so I wandered around a bit before returning. The white house in question consist of various part of american history, starting with all the past president.

The founding myth is depicted with a bridge, also certain rooms are tilted a few degrees. Afterwards they depict the steam age with a vaccum cleaner in the background, and a train, the side simulating a train ride. Near the end the moon landing was depicited with a lego toy on a string.

It felt like a intresting view of america. I also took a few rides, one was a looping airplane. The view of the city was really nice, since it is located between Nara and Osaka

The hike down was non eventful, I saw a few shrines through. A sign promise a path to a waterfall, however the mountain was really steep so I did not bother. The numbers of stairs is just absurd.

Nara is known for its deers, through I wasn’t prepared for how hostile they are. I got some deer biscuit, and some deer just arrive and rip the entire package. They ended up on the floor and I got a sight of the deer competing to eat it.

Day 4 reflection

So for Day 4 I am actually staying in kyoto, that was the plan. Dinner was nice. Through tonight isnt ending very early…

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 4
entry.content:
Trip to Wakayama

On day 4, with the great weather, I decided its time to push the button. Tomogashima Island ahve been of interest since I saw it in some anime, also abandoned places are kinda my thing so I went ahead with this. I booked a ferry at 0900, so I got up early, ate the cup noodles I made a few days ago and too the train.

There is a convenient train towards Kada town, where I have to walk to the harbor. It feels like a quiet Japanese town, very different from the Osaka city

During the morning, the ship was delayed and potentially cancelled. I was worried but an hour later it was fine and the boat went ahead.

Trip AT the island

The island I visit was a former world war 2 defense port. There is no local population, I went past a few abandoned buildings. I met a new friend over here, we still talk occasionally. It was an exciting hike, through the right side is sealed off for safety reason. I walked most of the island.

I actually miss the ferry back, it was kinda too full. I spoke to my new friends and they are much older than me. I guess most people my age don’t have the money or the recklessness to commit to a solo trip. Through I panic for a moment I did learn to go with the flow.

Day 3 reflection

Since those two is of privacy minded I can’t put this online.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 3
entry.content:
Day 3 Standout

Day 3 starts with a rainy day, where I make some changes to my plans instead. It was nice to witness the city in a different weather

Osaka Aquarium and car museum

The first location I visited was Osaka Aquarium, I have seen it in an anime before so I was kinda curious.

Overall the aquarium was really nice, I paid extra to enter the feeding area. The caretakers are very knowledgeable and speak some english. Q:All these animals how do they coexist in the same tent A:We keep the animals well fed, the animals do not attack each other if they are full. Q:Is this why the divers are safe in these animals A:Yes, the animals recognize the divers as a food provider. I also see a few of the divers feeding them and a trainer. The backstage experience is worth the cost if I want to ask questions. Unfortunately I am not a big marine entuaist so I don’t have much to ask, however if someone is really curious this is worth the cost. The rest of the place is well maintained and the envrioment looks exciting. I will go again in the future.

Looking at the map I noticed a old car museum, the Glion Museum. I do like cars, my dad like cars, it is located at this factory like place. I had to do a double take to ensure I arrived at the right location.

The entire place consist of many cars, from the start of transport with horses, to the old cars. Most of the description are in japanese, but it looks pretty nice. It is a small nice museum for an hour or two.

Umeda Sky

A view of Osaka from Umeda Sky has two distinctive side, one side where the river is visible while the other side is more modern. It was included in the Osaka card and it is worth the short time to have a nice view of Osaka. There was also an art museum nearby.

Reflection of Day 3

Day 3 is a lot lighter, but it was a pretty enjoyable experience. I think the highlights is seeing how different nation can do the same thing(like an aquarium) differently and still be enjoyable. Osaka Aquarium is definetly the highlight and I learn a bit about marine life. Umeda Sky is a nice overview of Osaka.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 2
entry.content:
Day 2 Standout

Today was a busy day, in the morning I will be visiting Osaka Castle, while in the evening and night I am visiting the Osaka World Expo. My professor did mention this and shown a few videos, since then I have been curious about it.

Osaka Castle

As someone who collect figures in Singapore, I was suprised to see such a museum, and of course I have to visit. I saw various figures and learn abit about its history, and saw the different type of figure from the older type to the more modern figures. At the start it is anime figures, later on it is also really cool to see the diorama with Godzilla and friends at the end. It is a pretty neat small museum part of Osaka Castle

I have always been interested in castles and stuff, so visiting the castle was a dream come true. Unfortuantely the interior of the castle is basically a museum, I was hoping to see a preserved castle.

However, the display are rich with history. Through I don’t really remember what I saw. I do recall that it was very crowded, also something about the Dutch. They are the first to be trading with Japan before the American open gate approach, intresting stuff.

A fun fact they told us during the river ride is that one of the stones is called the “demon face”, where it is placed to ward off evil. I can see the stone while on the river. It is amazing how this castle survived the second world war, there is even a dispaly of a burn damage from an american bomb.

When leaving for the World Expo I saw that there is this model of Osaka Castle and the surrounding. Really liked this. I learnt quite a bit about Osaka Castle and the history of Japan. The parks are also great.

Osaka World Expo

The highlight of the day is the 2025 Osaka World Expo. I heard my professor mention this and I knew I wanted to see this. It feels amazing to attend something that started in 1897 and is still alive, many nation come across the world to exhibit their nation here.

I booked a night ticket as I thought that it will be less crowded, and easier to get in. Clearly I am mistaken since not only does everyone have the same idea as me, many pavilion closed their doors early. I picked the Japan Pavilion for the lottery and got it, so I went and these are how it went

Malaysia Pavilion

The first Pavilion I visit is that of Malaysia, it has some nice exhibit and life size figures depicting a Malay market. The above image is that of the goals to have a smart city. I wish my neighbors a success.

As the pavilion that informed me of this event I had to visit my home nation Pavilion. It takes the form of a big red dot, honestly visually it looks unique, and it is better than some nations which are a literal box.

Honestly the Singapore Pavilion is very generic, maybe because I am from Singapore but I don’t really remember anything intresting. Will avoid this in the future.

The first thing in the Japanese Pavilion is that of their recycling efforts, and a timestamp of the various decay of their biodegradable material. This was an intresting choice and while I heard of it, seeing how it degenerate slowly is intresting.

The center of the pavilion marks two queue, one where you can touch a meteorite and the other where you go your way. Of course I went with the queue, not everyday a meteorite shows up. Standing at this circular spot,it is very peaceful and I was able to take in the scenery(becaues my phone data is limited I didn’t use it)

Next there are some depicting the animal products, it really makes me think how much water we use to raise certain product. I am more appericate of the meat product and water usage. I also now understand why were meat a luxury product, in the past animals must be hard to raise. From this I will try not to waste food.

The final room is a mirror room of DNA strains, it was an intresting display and the chaos that results in humans. It is visually stunning and I stayed a bit.

Overall I really enjoyed the Japan Pavilion, there is a clear focus on enviromentalism, which is an important topic, and Japan effort towards this. I guess it makes sense Japan will not be advertising themself since this takes place in Japan, so it is nice to see the nation commitment.

This was an intresting pavilion which I suspect is the Brazil pavilion. It features inflatable trees made of recycable material and changing color, it feels like an abstract art piece. At the back a single movie is looping talking about the country. I suspect this is Brazil? Through I am not very sure.

I stayed through and sitting in this enviroment also helps me take in the enviroment. Despite being on a rush to get to places, I find myself taking time to rest. This was an intrestng pavilion.

Additional Pavilion I visit was Bangladesh before I climb to the circular wooden circle that define the expo.

At the end I was present as the place close, there was a drone show. Pictures cannot do it justice, but it depicts unity and values. Viewing the pavilion downstairs looks beautiful

Day 2 Reflection

Of all things what I learn the most from these two days is to sit back and relax, to not rush from place to place. I really enjoyed sitting and soaking in the envrioment.
entry.summary: Day 2 Standout Today was a busy day, in the morning I will be visiting Osaka Castle, while in the evening and night I am visiting the Osaka World Expo. My professor did mention this and shown a few videos, since then I have been curious about it.

entry.title: Osaka, Japan Day 1
entry.content:
Background

In Singapore there is very strong japanese cultural influence, Doeramon plays here every Saturday, recently I found out about a budget airline. I decided to go on this trip to prepare for my long term exchange in europe. I find it suitable as Japan doesnt have english as its main language and is more affordable as well.

Day 1 Standout - Cup Noodle Museum and Ikeda Memorial park

As a student I had cup noodle frequently, Japanese cup noodles are always more expensive and considered prenium. So of course I had to visit the cup noodles. Over here I even get to make a cup noodle. Seeing the full process is intresting. The process is roughly as following:

  1. Place the instant noodle as well as the ingreidents
  2. Lid sealers are used that press a foil that stick to the cup.

Very near to the cup noodle factory, this park exist. I decided to pay it a visit as there isnt much time. I thought it was beautiful, I visited the remnants of a castle and had a view of the city. This spot was far away from the city, suprising I notice there was a wheelchair friendly features at this park.

I walked around Dotonbori, it was really crowded. I visited a few stores and had aomse Takoyaki and it is better than anything in Singapore. Suprising they have a Chinese menu as well, through it did not help because I cannot read Chinese.

Day 1 Reflection

Day 1 marks when I settle down, I stayed at a hostel where we each have our own room and a shared common area. I visited a few places and enjoyed myself.
entry.summary: Background In Singapore there is very strong japanese cultural influence, Doeramon plays here every Saturday, recently I found out about a budget airline. I decided to go on this trip to prepare for my long term exchange in europe. I find it suitable as Japan doesnt have english as its main language and is more affordable as well.
